content.problemStatement:
Ridbit is given an array a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​. He needs to find the number of ways to divide the array into contiguous subarrays such that:


Each element of the sequence aaa belongs to exactly one of the subarrays.
There is an integer mmm such that the MEX of every subarray is equal to mmm. The MEX of a sequence is the smallest non-negative integer which does not occur in this sequence.
Help Ridbit with this task.
constraints: 0≤ai≤N0 \leq a_i \leq N0≤ai​≤N for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of ways to divide the sequence, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
